scenarios.label: Florida LMB lake/pond warm stained surface-open calm/aggressive
scenarios.expected: Warm southern surface-open bass: frogs, topwaters, sliders/poppers, and reaction/swim tools should all be plausible.
biological_review_notes: Surface is legal and biologically important.
Frog lure/fly should remain candidates/finalists/pickable without being forced every day.
Frog visibility held: candidates 7/7, finalists 7/7, picks 2/7.

scenarios.label: Florida LMB lake/pond warm stained windy/reaction
scenarios.expected: Wind should push reaction baits, while surface/frog absence is acceptable only when surface blocking applies.
biological_review_notes: Windy band intentionally blocks surface in current shaping.
Spinnerbait/bladed-jig/lipless style influence should be visible.
Wind reaction chosen on 4/7 dates; surface blocked on 7/7 dates.

scenarios.label: Northern/Appalachian LMB cold winter lake/pond
scenarios.expected: Cold-water bass should lean slower/deeper; narrower output is acceptable when the row is honestly constrained.
biological_review_notes: Bottom/mid slow winter posture is expected.
No topwater pressure expected.

scenarios.label: Smallmouth summer lake/pond crawfish-oriented
scenarios.expected: Smallmouth lake output should include craw/tube/ned/jig logic and allow the new warmwater crawfish fly to appear.
biological_review_notes: Crawfish forage is authored on this row.
Warmwater crawfish fly visibility is a key row-inclusion check.
warmwater_crawfish_fly picked on 2/7 dates.

scenarios.label: Smallmouth summer river
scenarios.expected: River-valid smallmouth craw/sculpin/baitfish presentations should appear without lake-only oddities.
biological_review_notes: River smallmouth flies should stay within river-eligible streamers/craw/sculpin logic.

scenarios.label: Northern pike summer lake/pond baitfish/perch
scenarios.expected: Pike output should feature large baitfish/reaction lures and pike streamers, including pike flash fly visibility.
biological_review_notes: Some pike streamer family concentration can be honest.
Pike flash fly is the targeted row-inclusion check.
pike_flash_fly picked on 3/7 dates.

scenarios.label: Northern pike summer river
scenarios.expected: River-valid pike baitfish/reaction presentations should appear; lake-only patterns must not leak in.
biological_review_notes: Pike flash fly should be valid and visible in the warm river row.
pike_flash_fly picked on 2/7 dates.

scenarios.label: Trout summer river clear/calm/subtle
scenarios.expected: Clear/calm trout should lean subtle and trout-appropriate; no warmwater bass/pike flies.
biological_review_notes: Clear subtle lure window should be visible where matching lures exist.
Warmwater fly IDs must not appear.

scenarios.label: Trout summer river mouse/topwater window
scenarios.expected: Mouse/topwater fly condition logic should visibly influence finalist or pick sets without invalid warmwater leakage.
biological_review_notes: Mouse fly condition window should be active when a surface slot is present.
trout_mouse_window active on 7/7 dates; mouse_fly picked on 2/7 dates.

scenarios.label: Cold winter trout river
scenarios.expected: Cold trout output can be narrower if it stays seasonally and species credible.
biological_review_notes: Do not force variety at the expense of cold-water posture.
